def solve(nums, queries)
  positions = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    positions[num] << i
  end

  ans = []
  unique_elements = positions.keys

  queries.each do |li, ri, threshold|
    candidates = []
    unique_elements.each do |x|
      arr = positions[x]
      low = li
      high = ri

      # Find first index >= low
      low_idx = arr.bsearch_index { |e| e >= low } || arr.size
      # Find first index > high
      high_idx = arr.bsearch_index { |e| e > high } || arr.size
      count = high_idx - low_idx

      if count >= threshold
        candidates << [x, count]
      end
    end

    if candidates.empty?
      ans << -1
    else
      # Sort by count descending, then by value ascending
      candidates.sort! { |a, b| 
        if a[1] != b[1]
          b[1] - a[1]
        else
          a[0] <=> b[0]
        end
      }
      ans << candidates[0][0]
    end
  end
  ans
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  positions = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, i|
    positions[num] << i
  end

  ans = []
  unique_elements = positions.keys

  queries.each do |li, ri, threshold|
    candidates = []
    unique_elements.each do |x|
      arr = positions[x]
      low = li
      high = ri

      # Find first index >= low
      low_idx = arr.bsearch_index { |e| e >= low } || arr.size
      # Find first index > high
      high_idx = arr.bsearch_index { |e| e > high } || arr.size
      count = high_idx - low_idx

      if count >= threshold
        candidates << [x, count]
      end
    end

    if candidates.empty?
      ans << -1
    else
      # Sort by count descending, then by value ascending
      candidates.sort! { |a, b| 
        if a[1] != b[1]
          b[1] - a[1]
        else
          a[0] <=> b[0]
        end
      }
      ans << candidates[0][0]
    end
  end
  ans
end
